originalTitle: Women secretly filmed, then ridiculed and abused online
title: Let's Be Safe and Kind Online!
content:
Hello, hello, my wonderful friends across Africa! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, ready to share something important with you today. We love to play, learn, and have fun, right? And sometimes, we use phones or computers to do exciting things like watch cartoons or learn new games. But just like playing outside, we need to remember some special rules to keep us safe when we are online.

Hello, Friends! What Are We Talking About Today?

We are going to talk about being safe when we see new people, and when we use the internet. The internet is like a very, very big library or a giant playground where lots of people from all over the world can meet and share things. It's an amazing place, but we need to be careful, just like we are careful when crossing the road or playing with new toys.

Friends in Kenya and Ghana

Sometimes, people meet others who might not be as kind as we hope. There are some grown-ups, our friends in countries like Kenya and Ghana, who told a news company called the BBC about something that made them feel very sad. A man came to them and started talking. Sometimes, he secretly took videos of them. This means he used a phone or a camera to film them, but he did not ask them if it was okay. He didn't say, "Can I please take a video of you?" And they didn't say "Yes."

What is 'Asking Permission'?

When you want to borrow your friend's toy, what do you do? You say, "Can I please play with your toy?" And your friend can say "Yes," or "No." That's called asking permission. It means you respect what someone wants. When someone takes a video or a picture of you, they should always ask you first. You have the right to say "Yes" or "No." Your body, your smile, your face – they are yours! No one should take a picture or video of you without your permission.

Sharing Things Online

After this man secretly took videos, he put them on the internet for everyone to see. He did not ask if it was okay to share them. And what made it even sadder and very unkind was that some people on the internet made fun of the grown-ups in the videos. They said mean things. This made the people in the videos feel very, very sad and upset. It's called being "ridiculed" or "abused online." It means people were very unkind with their words. Did you know? The internet is connected by lots and lots of wires, even under the ocean! This helps people talk to each other from far away.

Why Is This Not Okay?

It is never okay for someone to secretly film you. It is never okay for someone to share your video or picture without your permission. And it is absolutely never okay for anyone to be unkind or make fun of others, whether it's in person or on the internet. Everyone deserves to be treated with kindness and respect. Your feelings are important. If something like this happens, it's not your fault at all. The person who did it is the one who was wrong.

What Can You Do to Be Safe?

Here are some super important tips to keep you safe and happy, especially when you are using the internet or meeting new people: **1. Always Talk to a Grown-Up You Trust:** If someone asks to take your picture or video, or if someone you don't know talks to you, always, always tell a grown-up you trust. This could be your mom, dad, gogo, teacher, or an auntie or uncle. They are there to help you! Did you know? In many African cultures, respecting elders and asking for guidance from grown-ups is very important! **2. Your 'Yes' and 'No' Are Important:** Remember, you can always say "No" if you don't want someone to take your picture or video. It's your right! If someone makes you feel uncomfortable, you can walk away and tell a grown-up. **3. Don't Share Your Secrets with Strangers:** On the internet, some people pretend to be friends, but they might be strangers. A stranger is someone you don't know. Never tell strangers your name, where you live, or what school you go to. Never send them pictures or videos of yourself. **4. Be Kind Online:** Just like you are kind to your friends at school, be kind when you are online. Don't say mean things, and don't share pictures or videos that would make someone else feel sad or bad. If you see someone being unkind online, tell a grown-up. Did you know? The first phone call was made over 140 years ago! Now we can make video calls and see our friends and family far away. **5. Ask Before You Share:** If you want to share a picture or video of yourself or a friend online, always ask a grown-up first. They can help you decide if it's safe and if it's kind. And remember to ask your friend too!

Why Being Kind Matters So Much

Being kind makes everyone feel good. When we respect each other's privacy – which means respecting their personal space and what belongs to them, like their pictures and videos – we show we care. We want everyone to feel safe and happy, not sad or scared. Did you know? There are over 54 countries in Africa, and each one has amazing stories and kind people!

Let's Keep Everyone Safe!

So, my wonderful friends, remember these important things: Always talk to a trusted grown-up. Your "yes" and "no" are powerful. Be careful with strangers, especially online. And always, always be kind. The internet can be a wonderful place for learning and fun, but we must use it wisely and safely. Let's all work together to make sure everyone feels safe and respected, both online and in person. Thank you for listening, and stay safe out there!
summary: Some grown-ups in Kenya and Ghana were filmed without asking permission and had their videos shared online, which made them sad, so we must learn to be safe and kind online and always talk to a trusted grown-up.
whyMatters: It is important because everyone deserves to feel safe and respected, and we need to know how to use the internet carefully and be kind to others, just like we are in real life.

originalTitle: Iran emergency workers search for survivors after deadly US-Israel attacks
title: Helping Hands in Hard Times Far Away
content:
Hello, Friends from Africa!

Hello, wonderful friends! Your kid-friendly reporter from Africa is here. Today, we want to talk about some very brave people who help others when big problems happen far, far away from us. It’s important to learn about being kind and helping, no matter where people live. We are like one big family on Earth!

Sometimes, sad things happen in the world. When troubles come, like a big storm, or when buildings get hurt, people need a lot of help. Today, we are learning about a country called Iran. It is very far away from Africa, across big oceans and lands. Imagine a map – Africa is one big part, and Iran is another part, quite a distance away.

Brave Helpers in Iran

In Iran, there are many brave people called "emergency workers." Have you ever seen a fire truck or an ambulance? Those are emergency workers! They are like superheroes without capes. Their job is to rush to places where people are in trouble and need help right away.

Recently, in a city in Iran called Qom, some sad things happened. Imagine if a building wasn't safe anymore, or if there was a loud noise and things got messy. When these kinds of troubles happened in Qom, some people got hurt. Sadly, six people even lost their lives. This made many people very, very sad.

The brave emergency workers immediately went to work. They put on their special clothes and got their tools. Their most important job was to search for people who might be stuck or hurt. They looked very carefully, using special equipment, hoping to find anyone who needed saving.

Did you know? Emergency workers train very hard, just like athletes! They learn how to stay safe themselves while helping others. They are very strong and smart!

Searching for Survivors

When sad events happen, like big accidents or troubles that hurt buildings, it can be very scary. People might get trapped. That’s why the emergency workers search for "survivors." A survivor is someone who has lived through a difficult situation. Finding a survivor means bringing hope and safety back.

The teams in Qom searched day and night. They moved big pieces of things carefully. They listened for any sound. They used their hands and special dogs to sniff for people. It is a very difficult job, but they never give up because they want to help everyone they can.

It's like a big treasure hunt, but instead of gold, they are looking for precious people. They work together, helping each other, making sure every corner is checked. This teamwork is so important when big problems happen.

Why Do We Care So Much?

You might be wondering, "Why are we talking about a place so far away?" Well, because we are all people on this planet! When someone is hurting in one part of the world, it is kind to care and understand. We learn about empathy – which means understanding how someone else feels. Even if we can't be there to help, we can send our good thoughts and learn from their bravery.

Did you know? There are many kinds of emergency workers! Some are doctors and nurses who heal people. Some are firefighters who put out fires. Some are police officers who keep everyone safe. They all work together!

These sad events are part of bigger problems that have been happening in Iran. For a long time, there have been troubles and difficult times. Because of these big problems, many, many people have been hurt or lost their lives in different places. It's a very big number, and it makes our hearts feel heavy.

Remembering Those Who Need Help

When we hear about so many people getting hurt, it reminds us that we all need to try our best to live in peace. Imagine if everyone was kind to each other, like you are with your friends at school. The world would be a much happier place.

The workers in Iran are not just looking for survivors; they are also helping the families who are sad. They are helping them find safety and giving them comfort. It’s about showing love and support when things are very hard.

Did you know? In many parts of Africa, we also have amazing emergency workers! They work hard every day to keep our communities safe, whether it's helping after a big rainstorm or if someone needs medical help.

Hope for Tomorrow

Even when things are sad, there is always hope. The bravery of the emergency workers gives us hope. The kindness of people helping each other gives us hope. We hope that one day, all the big problems in places like Iran will stop, and everyone can live in peace and safety.

So, next time you see an ambulance or a fire truck, remember those brave helpers in Iran and all over the world. Remember how important it is to be kind, to help others, and to wish for peace for everyone, everywhere. Let's send our good thoughts to the people in Iran and hope for brighter days ahead.

Thank you for listening, little changemakers! Until next time, stay kind and stay curious!
summary: Brave emergency workers in Iran are helping people after some sad events, showing how important it is for everyone to be kind and help each other, no matter how far away they are.
whyMatters: This story matters because it teaches us about kindness, bravery, and how important it is for people to help each other when bad things happen, even in faraway places like Iran. It reminds us that we all hope for peace and safety for everyone in the world.
